skill.name: nature-citation
skill.description: Find Nature, Science, and Cell-family citation candidates for manuscript claims, then export reference-manager files for EndNote, RIS, or Zotero review.
skill.summary: Finds Nature/CNS citation candidates for manuscript text and exports review-ready reference files.
skill.author: Yuan1z skill, refactored into static/dynamic layers

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 308 static findings and found no confirmed malicious intent or prompt injection. Most static hits are false positives from Markdown backticks, documentation text, citation-key identifiers, CSS names, and non-security hash use. The publishable risks are expected Crossref network queries using manuscript-derived terms, local file output, and a moderate local HTML XSS risk from innerHTML rendering of selected metadata.

medium_findings.title: Manuscript-derived search terms sent to Crossref
medium_findings.description: The script turns user manuscript segments into search queries and sends them to the Crossref API as bibliographic query parameters. This is expected for citation discovery, but it can disclose unpublished research topics or draft claim wording to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly builds queries from segment text and passes them to fetch_crossref, which sends query.bibliographic to api.crossref.org. This is legitimate functionality, but the privacy impact is clear for confidential drafts.

medium_findings.title: Local HTML review page uses innerHTML with metadata fields
medium_findings.description: The generated citation browser clears a selected-reference panel with innerHTML and then renders selected item title, journal, and year through a template literal. If malicious HTML reached those metadata fields, it could execute when the local review page is opened. The impact is limited because source data comes from Crossref metadata and the page is a local artifact.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The innerHTML pattern is confirmed and selected records are parsed from embedded metadata. Several earlier fields are HTML-escaped, so exploitation would require malicious metadata to survive into the selected item object.

low_findings.title: Expected local file reads and output writes
low_findings.description: The CLI reads user-provided text, claim, and DOI files, then writes reference exports and optional review artifacts under a user-selected output path. This is normal for a citation export tool, but users should avoid pointing output paths at sensitive locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Path handling and file IO are visible in the CLI implementation and match the documented export workflow. No evidence found of secret-file harvesting or hidden filesystem scanning.

low_findings.title: Static command-execution and weak-crypto hits are false positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick findings are Markdown inline code and documented shell examples, not executable Ruby code. The weak-crypto finding in the Python script is SHA-1 used for stable non-security identifiers, not password storage, signatures, or encryption.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no subprocess, os.system, shell=True, eval, or exec usage in the script. The hash function is used only to build stable URN-like IDs for citation records.

low_findings.title: Certificate, key, reconnaissance, and obfuscation alerts are benign identifiers or UI text
low_findings.description: The sensitive-file alerts refer to citation keys, dictionary keys, or JavaScript map keys, not private keys or certificates. System reconnaissance alerts align with CSS layout names or documentation examples, and no evidence found of host profiling, credential access, or evasion logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations use ordinary citation-record identifiers, UI styling, or evaluation prompt text. No evidence found of credential material, encrypted payloads, or attempts to inspect the host system.

dangerous_patterns.title: innerHTML assignment in generated browser artifact
dangerous_patterns.description: The review browser uses innerHTML for selected-reference rendering. Safer DOM textContent assignment would reduce local HTML injection risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The assignment is explicit in the browser template. The context limits severity because the artifact is generated locally for review.

content.value_statement: Academic writers need focused citations without mixing unrelated journals into a manuscript draft. This skill segments claims, searches strict Nature, Science, and Cell-family sources, and exports review-ready reference files.
actual_capabilities: Splits manuscript text into stable citable segments such as S001 and S002.
Builds English literature search queries from manuscript claims, including Chinese-language input.
Searches Crossref metadata for Nature Portfolio, AAAS Science family, and Cell Press candidates.
Filters candidate papers by strict journal scope and optional publication year limits.
Exports EndNote ENW, RIS, or Zotero RDF reference files.
Can generate HTML, TSV, JSON, and Markdown review artifacts when requested.
limitations: Candidate citations are metadata-level results until a user checks the abstract or full text.
The script sends manuscript-derived search terms to Crossref, so confidential drafts need caution.
It does not retrieve full text or prove that a paper supports a claim.
It is limited to configured Nature, Science, and Cell-family journal scopes.

use_cases.title: Support a manuscript paragraph
use_cases.description: Find scoped citation candidates for each claim in a draft paragraph and export a reference file for manual review.

use_cases.title: Screen citations for a review article
use_cases.description: Process multiple sections in batches and build a segment-to-reference map for high-level literature screening.

use_cases.title: Prepare bilingual citation support
use_cases.description: Use Chinese instructions and manuscript notes while searching with English scientific concepts and strict journal filters.

prompt_templates.title: Add basic citations
prompt_templates.prompt: Add Nature/CNS citation candidates to this paragraph and export an EndNote file: [paste paragraph].
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one short paragraph.

prompt_templates.title: Limit by journal scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Find Nature Portfolio citation candidates for these claims only. Include a segment-to-reference map: [paste claims].
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused search for one publisher family.

prompt_templates.title: Use date filters
prompt_templates.prompt: Find CNS and sister-journal citations from 2020 to 2026 for this manuscript section, then export RIS: [paste section].
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with publication year limits and RIS export.

prompt_templates.title: Process a long draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Process this long manuscript in batches, generate the HTML review browser, and export unique Zotero RDF records: [paste or attach text].
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for long manuscripts and manual screening.

output_examples.input: Find Nature/CNS references for a paragraph about CRISPR screens identifying cancer dependencies.
output_examples.output: The response groups the paragraph into numbered segments, lists candidate papers for each segment, labels them as metadata-only candidates, and provides an EndNote export path.

output_examples.input: Add Nature Portfolio citations to a Chinese manuscript paragraph and include an HTML review page.
output_examples.output: The response keeps Chinese review notes, uses English scientific search terms, reports the strict Nature Portfolio scope, and links the local review browser first.

output_examples.input: Export confirmed DOI records as RIS for EndNote.
output_examples.output: The response fetches metadata for the supplied DOI list, writes a RIS file, and warns users not to invent missing bibliographic fields.

best_practices: Verify every candidate against the abstract or publisher page before citing it.
Use year filters when the field is fast-moving or the manuscript needs recent literature.
Enable review artifacts for long drafts so users can screen candidates in the HTML browser.
anti_patterns: Do not cite a paper only because its title contains related terms.
Do not use confidential manuscript text with live Crossref search unless external disclosure is acceptable.
Do not broaden beyond the configured journal families without telling the user.

faq.question: Which journals does this skill search?
faq.answer: It searches configured Nature Portfolio, AAAS Science family, and Cell Press journal scopes. It can also restrict results to flagship journals.

faq.question: Does it prove that a citation supports my sentence?
faq.answer: No. It marks candidates as metadata-level until you check the abstract, publisher page, or full text.

faq.question: What export formats are supported?
faq.answer: It supports EndNote ENW, RIS, and Zotero RDF. Optional review artifacts include HTML, TSV, JSON, and Markdown.

faq.question: Can it handle Chinese input?
faq.answer: Yes. It can keep Chinese review notes while using English scientific search terms for metadata search.

faq.question: Is internet access required?
faq.answer: Yes for live citation discovery. The script queries Crossref and can fetch DOI metadata from the same API.

faq.question: Is it safe for unpublished work?
faq.answer: Use caution. Search terms derived from the manuscript are sent to Crossref, so confidential drafts may need offline or manual handling.
